recommendation: Keep the built-in Demo local-only, read-only, and outside Firebase and myTrips.
rationale: The Demo factory returns an in-memory view model.
The preview contract delegates callbacks and has no persistence authority.
assumptions: The accepted Demo ADR remains authoritative.
options: Persist a Demo room, which conflicts with the accepted boundary.
risks: Future integration could accidentally reuse a production save path; retain explicit boundary tests.
testsRequired: Assert Demo open does not call Firebase writes.

evidence.claim: The Demo boundary is recorded as local-only.
Demo data is created by a local factory.

unknowns: Real-device presentation remains outside this persistence question.
